title: jasminum flavovirens
intro: Jasminum flavovirens, also known as the golden jasmine or yellow jasmine, is a flowering plant native to the Himalayan and subtropical regions of Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Jasminum flavovirens include its ability to alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to treat various health conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
